2606.23888.title: E-MRL: Cross-view Aligned Evidence-driven Multimodal Reinforcement Learning for Reliable 3D Tumor Analysis
2606.23888.paper_summary: While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) show great promise in volumetric medical report generation, they frequently suffer from visual hallucinations and a lack of grounding in 3D CT data. Current Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) strategies typically optimize text fidelity alone, essentially rewarding correct diagnoses derived from language priors rather than genuine visual perception. To address this, we propose cross-view aligned Evidence-driven Multimodal Reinforcement Learning (Evidence-MRL, noted as E-MRL), a reliable RL reasoning framework that formulates the generation process as a Markov Decision Process of "diagnosis-localization-verification". Unlike standard approaches, our model is explicitly trained to identify a "key evidence slice" alongside the global diagnostic report, grounding its findings in verifiable visual evidence. Crucially, we introduce a novel cross-view consistency reward, which validates the semantic alignment between the golden-standard report and a local visual re-query of the selected key slice, providing additional rewards for correctly-localized reasoning. Experiments on large-scale 3D CT tumor datasets demonstrate that E-MRL significantly reduces hallucinations and improves diagnostic accuracy compared to SFT and RL baselines, offering a clinically interpretable solution for visually-grounded and tumor analysis.
2606.23888.paper_summary_zh: 雖然視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在體積醫學報告生成中顯示出巨大的潛力，但它們經常受到視覺幻覺和缺乏對3D CT數據的基礎支持的困擾。當前的監督微調（SFT）和強化學習（RL）策略通常僅優化文本的真實性，基本上是獎勵從語言先驗中得出的正確診斷，而非真正的視覺感知。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了跨視角對齊的證據驅動多模態強化學習（Evidence-MRL，記作E-MRL），這是一個可靠的強化學習推理框架，將生成過程公式化為“診斷-定位-驗證”的馬爾可夫決策過程。與標準方法不同，我們的模型明確訓練以識別“關鍵證據切片”，並與全球診斷報告一起，將其發現基於可驗證的視覺證據。關鍵的是，我們引入了一種新穎的跨視角一致性獎勵，該獎勵驗證了黃金標準報告與所選關鍵切片的局部視覺重新查詢之間的語義對齊，並為正確定位的推理提供額外獎勵。在大規模3D CT腫瘤數據集上的實驗表明，E-MRL顯著減少了幻覺並提高了診斷準確性，相較於SFT和RL基準，為視覺基礎和腫瘤分析提供了一個臨床可解釋的解決方案。
2606.23888.author: Sijing Li et.al.
2606.23888.authors: Sijing Li, Zhongwei Qiu, Zhuoya Wang, Boxiang Yun, Zhenyu Yi, Jianwei Xu, Wenqiao Zhang, Yingda Xia, Ling Zhang
